1.choiceText: Me dirijo a usted para informarle que...

grade.selectedChoiceText: Me dirijo a usted para informarle que...
best: Me dirijo a usted para informarle que...

feedback.natural: Me dirijo a usted para informarle que... is the best answer. This is the standard, highly natural formula for formal business correspondence.
feedback.alternatives: A. Te escribo para decirte que...: This is perfectly correct for a friend, but far too informal for a client. C. Estoy escribiendo para informar que...: This sounds like a literal translation from English and lacks the expected formal phrasing. D. Le aviso de que...: While grammatically acceptable, 'avisar' can sound a bit abrupt or casual for an opening statement.
feedback.chunk: Practice Me dirijo a usted para informarle que... as a reusable Spanish expression for this kind of real situation.

best: Tenemos que llegar a un acuerdo.

feedback.natural: Tenemos que llegar a un acuerdo. is the best answer. The exact natural collocation for 'reaching an agreement' is 'llegar a un acuerdo'.
feedback.alternatives: A. Necesitamos hacer un acuerdo.: In Spanish, we do not use 'hacer' (make) with agreements in this context. C. Hay que tomar un acuerdo.: You can 'tomar una decisión' (make a decision), but you don't 'tomar un acuerdo'. D. Debemos coger un acuerdo.: 'Coger' is never used for abstract concepts like agreements.
feedback.chunk: Practice Tenemos que llegar a un acuerdo. as a reusable Spanish expression for this kind of real situation.
